title: release/19.x: [InstCombine] Drop noundef attributes in `foldCttzCtlz` (#116718)
summary:
The developers generalized the optimization by recognizing that the `noundef` attribute on the cttz/ctlz intrinsic was unnecessarily restrictive. The original pattern only matched `ctlz` with `is_zero_poison=false`, but the underlying logic—that shifting by cttz/ctlz with a zero operand is safe to convert to `is_zero_poison=true`—applies regardless of `noundef`.

The key insight was that when the shift amount is zero (via `match(Op1, m_Zero())`), the `is_zero_poison` flag can be safely set to `true` without changing semantics, as zero shift amounts are well-defined. To enable this, they added `II.dropUBImplyingAttrsAndMetadata()` before modifying the operand, which strips `noundef` and other UB-implying attributes that would otherwise block the transformation.

This approach demonstrates a useful pattern: when generalizing an optimization, identify and drop overly conservative attributes that don’t affect correctness under the specific preconditions. The fix ensures the optimization applies to both `ctlz` and `cttz`, with or without `noundef`, making it more widely applicable while preserving safety.
